management.mowing: Can tolerate very low greens mowing when grown and conditioned properly
management.water: Requires careful moisture management; avoid chronic wetness and severe dry-down
management.fertility: Use spoon-feeding and avoid large nitrogen swings on greens
management.cultivation: Manage thatch and organic matter with regular cultivation

creeping_bentgrass.diagnostic_notes: On greens, distinguish disease from wilt, localized dry spot, traffic, mower injury, and PGR stress before recommending pesticides.

management.water: Avoid extremes; shallow roots make wilt and heat stress common
management.fertility: Low nitrogen increases anthracnose risk; excessive nitrogen can worsen other issues
management.pgr: Seedhead suppression and growth regulation programs are timing-sensitive

annual_bluegrass.diagnostic_notes: Poa annua decline often has multiple causes: heat, root stress, anthracnose, traffic, and moisture extremes.

management.mowing: Performs well at moderate lawn/sports heights
management.water: Can go dormant under drought; avoid frequent shallow irrigation
management.fertility: Fall fertility is important for cool-season recovery

kentucky_bluegrass.diagnostic_notes: Ring symptoms in Kentucky bluegrass should raise root disease and drought-stress differentials.

management.mowing: Avoid mowing too low; higher heights improve stress tolerance
management.fertility: Avoid heavy summer nitrogen during brown patch pressure

tall_fescue.diagnostic_notes: Brown patch is common in humid summer weather, but drought, dull mower injury, and fertility stress can mimic thinning.

weaknesses: Limited cold tolerance in some sites

management.mowing: Maintains quality under frequent mowing when not heat-stressed
management.water: Avoid prolonged leaf wetness during hot humid conditions
management.fertility: Avoid excessive soluble nitrogen during gray leaf spot pressure

perennial_ryegrass.diagnostic_notes: Rapid blighting in hot humid weather on ryegrass should raise gray leaf spot and Pythium differentials.

strengths: Lower mowing tolerance for naturalized uses

management.mowing: Avoid aggressive low mowing in stress periods
management.fertility: Do not overfertilize; low to moderate fertility is usually better
management.traffic: Limit traffic where fine fescue is used for shade/low-input areas

fine_fescue.diagnostic_notes: Before recommending herbicides, check fine fescue tolerance because some products are more restrictive.

management.mowing: Match mowing height to cultivar, use, and growth rate
management.water: Generally lower water use than cool-season turf but still needs irrigation under drought
management.fertility: Fertilize primarily during active growth; taper before dormancy
management.cultivation: Cultivation and thatch management support high-traffic recovery

bermudagrass.diagnostic_notes: Spring dead spot symptoms show in spring but preventive planning is fall-based.

management.mowing: Avoid scalping; adjust height around spring transition
management.fertility: Avoid excessive nitrogen, especially heading into large patch risk periods
management.cultivation: Manage thatch to reduce stress and disease risk

zoysiagrass.diagnostic_notes: Large patch risk increases in cool wet shoulder seasons; symptoms can appear after infection periods.

management.mowing: Maintain appropriate higher lawn heights; avoid scalping
management.water: Avoid drought stress but do not create chronically wet disease-prone turf
management.herbicides: Check turf tolerance carefully before recommending herbicides

st_augustinegrass.diagnostic_notes: Many herbicide labels restrict St. Augustinegrass; verify turf safety before product recommendations.

management.fertility: Avoid excessive nitrogen; centipedegrass is low-input
management.traffic: Avoid heavy traffic due to slow recovery

centipedegrass.diagnostic_notes: Overfertilization and unsuitable pH are common centipedegrass decline contributors.
